architecture.summary: An LLM agent initialized with a role receives observations and valid actions from text-based game environments, along with a symbolic module. The agent processes these inputs to select and execute actions within the game, forming a perception-decision-action loop where the LLM reasons over symbolic task constraints (math, map reading, sorting, common sense) and returns structured action choices to the environment.

biblio.title: Large Language Models Are Neurosymbolic Reasoners
biblio.venue: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence

claims.dataset: text-based games (math, map reading, sorting, common sense)
claims.statement: LLM agent achieves an average performance of 88% across all tasks
Our method significantly enhances the capability of LLMs as automated agents for symbolic reasoning
LLM agent is effective in text-based games involving symbolic tasks
